zh.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam是Uilliam的女兒，於大約1130年出生在蘇格蘭。她和John de Bisset至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam是Uilliam的女儿，于大约1130年出生在苏格兰。她和John de Bisset至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: nic
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Xiaosong, Cui, Dawei, Fei, Fen, Feng, Geng, Hui, Hui Ling, Huiming, Jin, Jing.
English (Essex and Middlesex): variant of   Nye  .

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 聶, meaning ‘whisper’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Nie (聶), the name of a fief (located in Henan province) granted to an official in the state of Wey (衛). (ii) from the placename Nie (聶), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to a son of the Duke Ding of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, reigned 1014–975  BC ).

variants.name: Lip
meanings: Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 聶, see   Nie   2.

variants.name: Nip
meanings: Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 聶, see   Nie   2.
Variant of   Nipp  .

variants.name: Nye
meanings: English (southern): from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atten eye, atten ye ‘at the island’ (Old English ēg, īeg ‘island’), becoming atte nye. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on an island or patch of firm ground surrounded by marsh, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Nye in Winscombe, Somerset.

English: alternatively, from a misdivision of the Middle English phrase atten eye, atten ee ‘at the river’ (Old English ēa ‘river’), becoming atte nee. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived near a river, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Neigh Bridge in Somerford Keynes, Wiltshire.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 聶, see   Nie   2.

nameMeanings.name: Uilliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Gill(i)am, Gillem, Old French Guillaume, a Parisian form of Will(i)am (see William ).

variants.name: Gill
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Avtar, Amritpal, Darshan, Hardip, Nirmal, Sohan, Ajit, Balwinder, Charan, Jasvir, Kewal, Navdeep.

English and Scottish: in northern England and Scotland sometimes from Middle English Gille, Old Norse Gilli, which is of Irish (Gaelic) origin (see below), and pronounced with a hard g. As a personal name it is not found after c. 1200.

English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English gille ‘deep glen, ravine’ (Old Norse (Norwegian) gil), pronounced with a hard g. The term is found mainly in northwestern England, where Norwegian Vikings settled.

variants.name: Gillim
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of   Gilliam  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Gwilliam
meanings: Welsh (Pembrokeshire and western England): from the Welsh personal name Gwillym, a form of Old French Guillaume (see Gilliam and William ).

variants.name: Gilham
meanings: English (southeastern): variant of   Gilliam  .

variants.name: Gillem
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of   Gilliam   and   Gwilliam  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Gillam
meanings: English: variant of   Gilliam  .
Welsh: Anglicized form of   Gwilliam  .

variants.name: Gilliom
meanings: English: variant of   Gilliam  .

nameMeanings.name: Dau
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Now used as a pet form of David , but originally of distinct origin, probably from an Old Celtic word dei ‘to shine’. See also Dewi .

en.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam was born about 1130, in Scotland as the daughter of Uilliam. She had at least 1 son with John de Bisset.

fr.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam est née environ 1130, à Écosse, fille de Uilliam. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec John de Bisset.

de.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam wurde um 1130 in Scotland, als Tochter von Uilliam geboren. Sie hatte mit John de Bisset mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam è nata il circa 1130 nel Scotland come figlia di Uilliam. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con John de Bisset.

ja.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliamは約1130年にスコットランドでUilliamの娘として生まれた。彼女はJohn de Bissetとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam(은)는 대략 1130년 스컷랜드에서 Uilliam의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 John de Bisset(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam nasceu aproximadamente 1130, em Scotland filha de Uilliam. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com John de Bisset.

ru.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam родилась около 1130 г. в местности Шотландия. Её отца звали Uilliam. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от John de Bisset.

es.lifeSummary: Dau nic Uilliam nació aproximadamente 1130, en Scotland como hija de Uilliam. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con John de Bisset.
